title: CDB vs LCI Post-Fixed Yield Calculator
meta_title: CDB vs LCI Post-Fixed Calculator (IR Regressive) | CalcForgeTools
meta_description: Compare the actual Net yields of standard Brazilian CDBs (taxed) versus LCI/LCA (tax-exempt) indexed dynamically to the CDI to verify which one returns more cash.

hero.title: CDB or LCI? Simulate Brazil's Net Yields
hero.subtitle: Discover exactly how the Brazilian regressive Income Tax bracket impacts your fixed-income returns, mathematically revealing their liquid potential over time.

related_guides.title: CDB vs LCI and LCA: The Ultimate Fixed-Income Investment Guide
related_guides.description: Learn to mathematically untangle tax exemptions and the FGC protection.

formula: CDB: Total - Regressive Tax | LCI: Total Exempt
formula_explanation: For CDI-indexed papers, interest compounds using equivalent daily rates ((1+Annual Rate)^(days/252)). The CDB deducts a specific Regressive Income Tax bracket (22.5% strictly down to 15%) from the profit margin. LCI/LCA linearly compounds purely up to its defined CDI offering without any tax deduction at source (0%).
example: If you hold R$ 10,000 in a CDB offering 110% CDI for exactly 360 days, your capital gains will be taxed at 20%. However, pushing the maturity date past 720 days drops the IR to a minimal tier of 15%, historically overtaking standard LCI yields even factoring in taxation offsets.

faq.question: How does the Regressive Income Tax (IR Regressivo) work on CDBs?
faq.answer: The Federal Revenue mitigates taxes strictly based on capital detention timelines: 1 to 180 days is subjected to 22.5%. 181 to 360 incurs 20%. Moving from 361 to 720 days demands 17.5%. Past an aggressive 720-day mark, the state only deducts 15% of total profitability margins.

faq.question: Aren't LCI/LCA bonds always mathematically superior?
faq.answer: A classic myth. Banks factor in the IR tax breaks, generally pricing LCIs with significantly lower CDIs (like 90%) compared to 110%-120% CDBs. Because the CDB gross interest grows exceptionally stronger with elevated multiplier factors, it commonly eclipses LCI net gains after 1 or 2 years when the IR brackets drop down to 17.5% bounds.

faq.question: What is the liquidity friction trap with LCIs?
faq.answer: Due to Brazilian Monetary Council (CMN) structural regulations issued recently, LCIs endure mandatory detention spans (commonly 9 months flat, or 12 for LCAs). Attempting rapid withdrawals is impossible. For liquid safety buffers (emergency funds), stick to immediate 'CDB Liquidez Diária' assets.
